# microservices.sh LLM Guide

Purpose: help LLM agents understand, inspect, and safely use microservices.sh module documentation.

## Product Summary

microservices.sh is a System Harness for agent-built Cloudflare apps. A developer uses their own agent, such as Claude, Codex, Cursor, or another MCP-compatible client, to compose verified Cloudflare-native modules into production app foundations with source-visible contracts, lockfiles, local checks, and approval-gated deploy plans.

## Canonical Module Documentation

Use these files before installing or modifying modules:

- `docs/modules/README.md` - module documentation index
- `docs/modules/module-spec-standard.md` - module documentation and contract standard
- `docs/modules/module-package-structure.md` - module folder layout and entrypoint standard
- `docs/modules/catalog.json` - compact machine-readable module catalog
- `docs/modules/auth.md` - Auth module reference
- `docs/modules/customer.md` - Customer module reference
- `docs/modules/booking.md` - Booking module reference
- `docs/modules/idempotency.md` - Idempotency module reference
- `docs/modules/payment-stripe.md` - planned Stripe payment provider module reference
- `docs/modules/email.md` - planned transactional email module reference
- `docs/modules/audit-log.md` - planned audit log module reference
- `docs/system-harness.md` - product definition and public workflow boundary
- `docs/quickstart-proof.md` - latest clean quickstart commands and verification result
- `docs/operator-pilot-boundary.md` - private-pilot boundary for Agent Center, Hermes, and Ads Manager, plus Marketing Research approval gates
- `docs/templates/README.md` - template documentation index
- `docs/templates/template-spec-standard.md` - standard for predefined repo-style templates
- `docs/templates/booking-sveltekit.md` - full Cloudflare SvelteKit booking app template spec and current shell

## Agent Rules

1. Prefer module docs and `module.json` over assumptions.
2. Inspect dependencies, secrets, env vars, resources, permissions, hooks, and upgrade notes before modifying code.
3. Never ask the user to paste secret values into chat.
4. Treat payment, email, auth, PII, webhooks, migrations, and deploy actions as approval-gated.
5. Use config and hooks before editing module internals.
6. Treat forked modules as user-owned code with manual or agent-assisted upgrades.
7. Generate patches or PRs against user-owned repos; do not hide source code changes.
8. Run `microservices upgrade <module-id> --plan --json` before changing locked module versions.
9. Run checks before preview or production deployment.

## Preferred Agent Flow

1. For a new app, start with `pnpm create microservices-app@latest <name> --template booking-sveltekit`.
2. Use the generated project CLI, `<pm> microservices`, as the public workflow surface. The root repo CLI is internal until its catalog is synced with the create-package templates.
3. Read `README.agent.md`, `docs/llms.txt`, and `microservices.lock.json` in generated projects.
4. Read `docs/modules/catalog.json`.
5. Read the relevant module Markdown files.
6. Read `docs/modules/module-package-structure.md` before changing module source.
7. Inspect the module via CLI first; use MCP when configured.
8. Produce an install/change plan.
9. Request approval for any gated side effects.
10. For version changes, produce an upgrade plan from `microservices.lock.json`.
11. Generate or update code.
12. Run checks.
13. Use `pnpm microservices deploy run --plan` for deploy planning/readiness.
14. Use live hosted deploy language only after the control plane has verified Worker/assets upload and route activation for the target environment.
15. Require explicit approval for production.
